question: Spread: CD Castellón (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for March 23 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CD Castellón" if CD Castellón win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CD Castellón vs. Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for March 23 at 3:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: CD Castellón enters this matchup as clear favorites per trader consensus, buoyed by their promotion to Segunda División and robust home form, while Cultural Leonesa struggles in Primera Federación with defensive frailties on the road. events.title: Chelsea FC vs. Port Vale FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FA Cup game, scheduled for April 4 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chelsea's overwhelming squad depth and home advantage at Stamford Bridge position them as heavy favorites against League Two side Port Vale in this EFL Cup third-round clash, with trader consensus implying over 90% win probability amid markets on scorelines and totals. Recent developments include Chelsea's rotated but potent lineup under Enzo Maresca—featuring returnees like Reece James and Nicolas Jackson—following their 4-2 Premier League victory over Brighton, while Port Vale sit mid-table in League Two after a gritty 1-1 draw with Bradford. No major injuries disrupt Chelsea's plans, but Port Vale's resilience in prior cup runs adds slight upset intrigue; expect high-pressing hosts to dominate possession and goals, tempered by cup unpredictability.

question: Chelsea FC vs. Port Vale FC: O/U 4.5
description:
In the upcoming FA Cup game between Chelsea FC and Port Vale FC, scheduled for April 4 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Chelsea FC and Port Vale FC combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on thefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. events.title: West Ham United FC vs. Leeds United FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FA Cup game, scheduled for April 4 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: West Ham's home strength and defensive solidity anchor trader consensus favoring them against Leeds, who sit atop the Championship but face a step up in class. Recent Hammers form shows three wins in five, bolstered by Jarrod Bowen's return from injury per official reports, while Leeds' automatic promotion push tempers aggression with rotation risks under Daniel Farke. Head-to-head history leans West Ham (4-2 aggregate last five), but Leeds' counterattack prowess and rest advantage post-midweek could spark upset value. Upcoming factors include West Ham's Europa League fatigue and Leeds' injury concerns at fullback, keeping implied probabilities tight around 55-45 for the hosts.

events.title: Manchester City FC vs. Liverpool FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FA Cup game, scheduled for April 4 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rodri's season-ending ACL injury remains the pivotal factor tilting trader consensus toward Liverpool in this high-stakes Premier League clash at Anfield, where City have won just once in the last five meetings. Liverpool's attacking firepower, led by Salah's 12 goals and 9 assists, exploits City's defensive vulnerabilities exposed in recent draws against Southampton and Villa. City's Haaland returns from injury but faces a rested Liverpool side with Diaz and Nunez in form after midweek Champions League wins. Momentum favors the Reds atop the table, though Guardiola's tactical edge and crowd rivalry could spark an upset, reflected in tight odds hovering around 45-55% Liverpool win probability.

In the upcoming FA Cup game between Manchester City FC and Liverpool FC, scheduled for April 4 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Manchester City FC and Liverpool FC combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on thefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Manchester City FC vs. Liverpool FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FA Cup game, scheduled for April 4 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rodri's season-ending ACL injury remains the pivotal factor tilting trader consensus toward Liverpool in this high-stakes Premier League clash at Anfield, where City have won just once in the last five meetings. Liverpool's attacking firepower, led by Salah's 12 goals and 9 assists, exploits City's defensive vulnerabilities exposed in recent draws against Southampton and Villa. City's Haaland returns from injury but faces a rested Liverpool side with Diaz and Nunez in form after midweek Champions League wins. Momentum favors the Reds atop the table, though Guardiola's tactical edge and crowd rivalry could spark an upset, reflected in tight odds hovering around 45-55% Liverpool win probability.

events.title: Southampton FC vs. Arsenal FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FA Cup game, scheduled for April 4 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's dominant recent form and squad depth drive trader consensus heavily toward the Gunners in their matchup against bottom-table Southampton, with implied probabilities reflecting an away win as the baseline expectation. Southampton sit 20th after a dismal start post-promotion, conceding 2.5 goals per game amid defensive injuries to Bednarek and Larios, while their attack lacks firepower without Sulemana fully fit. Arsenal, third in the standings, boast an unbeaten run across nine matches, bolstered by Saka and Rice returning from minor knocks per official reports. Key factors include Arsenal's strong away record (W5 D2 L1) and Southampton's home woes, though Saints' desperation could spark an upset; monitor final lineups for shifts.

events.title: FC Hermannstadt vs. FC Botoşani
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Romania SuperLiga game, scheduled for Sunday, March 22, 2026 between FC Hermannstadt and FC Botoşani.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hermannstadt's home advantage at Municipal Stadium anchors trader sentiment in this razor-thin Liga I clash, with Botoșani's resilient away form and recent draw-heavy streak keeping odds bunched near 44-45%. Both mid-table sides sit just four points apart in the standings after 15 matches, each nursing one win in their last five—Hermannstadt's 1-1 vs. CFR Cluj and Botoșani's gritty 0-0 at Rapid București underscoring defensive parity. No major injuries reported on official lists, but Hermannstadt's forward Cosmin Bîrlădeanu faces suspension, potentially blunting attack, while Botoșani's midfield anchor Mamadou Thiam returns from international duty. Head-to-head splits even (two draws last three meetings) amplify uncertainty, reflecting crowd wisdom on a probable stalemate.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming Saudi Professional League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Al Fayha Saudi Club and Al Ahli Saudi Club.

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Ahli Saudi Club holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 50.5% implied probability for their Saudi Pro League clash against Al Fayha, but bunched odds underscore Al Fayha's home resilience and Al Ahli's occasional away lapses. question: Will London have less than 20mm of precipitation in March?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors 30-40mm of precipitation in London for March, reflecting Met Office long-range forecasts indicating below-average rainfall across southeast England, driven by persistent high-pressure systems and a positive North Atlantic Oscillation phase suppressing Atlantic storms. Historical March averages hover around 44mm from 1991-2020 baselines at Heathrow, but recent winter deluges have given way to drier signals in ECMWF ensemble models, with 70% of members projecting under 50mm totals. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Chennaiyin FC vs. Inter Kashi FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Indian Super League game, scheduled for April 7 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chennaiyin FC's strong home form at Chennai's Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium anchors trader consensus favoring them against Inter Kashi FC in this ISL matchup, with recent 2-1 and 3-0 home wins boosting implied probabilities. Chennaiyin sit mid-table with momentum from three unbeaten games, led by attackers like Jordan Murray, while Inter Kashi languish near the bottom after four straight losses away, conceding heavily due to defensive lapses. No major injuries reported officially, but Inter Kashi's midfield absences from suspension could widen the gap; historical ISL trends show home sides winning 60% of such fixtures, underscoring rest advantages and crowd support as key swing factors.

question: X Money released by April 3, 2026?
description:
Elon Musk posted on X that X Money, X’s digital payment system will launch early public access next month (See: https://x.com/elonmusk/status/2031363107839438939).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if X Money is launched by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

X Money must be launched and publicly accessible to qualify, including via open beta, open rolling waitlist signups, or any other form of early public access launch. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. 

The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced by X as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official platform.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from X; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: X's aggressive push for money transmitter licenses across 34 U.S. states as of late October has boosted trader optimism for an imminent 𝕏 Money launch, with market-implied odds hovering around 60% for rollout by year-end despite no official product debut yet. Elon Musk's July confirmation of peer-to-peer payments "later this year" fuels speculation, but delays in key states like New York and California—requiring BitLicense approval—pose risks, mirroring regulatory hurdles faced by rivals like PayPal. Competitive pressure from Venmo and Cash App intensifies, as X aims for a WeChat-style everything app integrating social, video, and finance. Watch for Q4 earnings or Musk's next X Spaces for timeline clues, with full U.S. coverage as the resolution threshold.

question: o1 FDV above $100M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of o1's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If o1 (https://x.com/o1_exchange) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: US escorts commercial ship through Hormuz by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States military or federal government officially announces that it will escort, is escorting, or has escorted, any commercial ship through the Strait of Hormuz by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

Escort refers to United States military or government personnel accompanying or actively providing protective overwatch for a specific commercial vessel or convoy during its transit through the Strait of Hormuz. Personnel do not need to be aboard the commercial vessel to qualify. Escort may occur from a separate naval vessel or from aerial assets assigned to accompany or protect the transit.

A qualifying announcement must be definitive. Suggestions, unconfirmed reports, or other non-definitive statements will not count.

A widespread consensus of credible reporting that United States military or government personnel have escorted at least one commercial vessel on a journey through the Strait of Hormuz will also be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government and military of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran's seizure of the Israeli-linked MSC Aries commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on April 13 has sharpened focus on threats to global oil shipping, with the U.S. condemning the action and enhancing Fifth Fleet patrols to deter further disruptions. No public U.S. Navy escort of merchant ships through the chokepoint has been confirmed since, amid routine security transits, but trader consensus implies caution on near-term overt operations given de-escalation signals from Tehran post its April 13-14 attack on Israel. Upcoming Israeli responses or additional Iranian maritime moves could shift dynamics, as Hormuz handles 20% of seaborne oil trade.

question: Spread: Al Ittihad Saudi Club (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Saudi Professional League game, scheduled for April 7 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Al Ittihad Saudi Club" if Al Ittihad Saudi Club win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "NEOM SC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on slstat.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Will Clavicular be Iran's next Supreme Leader by March 31st?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Clavicular is officially named as the next Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic of Iran (or a clear direct replacement title for the role) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution of this market will be based on the first official announcement of the next Supreme Leader of Iran after Mojtaba Khamenei. 

If the position of Supreme Leader is formally abolished — including instances in which the Islamic Republic of Iran ceases to exist, is replaced, or no longer maintains the office of Supreme Leader — this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the government of Iran. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Clavicular be Iran's next Supreme Leader by March 31st?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Clavicular is officially named as the next Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic of Iran (or a clear direct replacement title for the role) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution of this market will be based on the first official announcement of the next Supreme Leader of Iran after Mojtaba Khamenei. 

If the position of Supreme Leader is formally abolished — including instances in which the Islamic Republic of Iran ceases to exist, is replaced, or no longer maintains the office of Supreme Leader — this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the government of Iran. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-absolute certainty that Clavicular will not become Iran's Supreme Leader by March 31st, driven primarily by the absence of any verifiable figure by that name in Iran's tightly controlled clerical establishment. Current Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, 84, shows no signs of imminent transition, with succession speculation centering on insiders like his son Mojtaba or President Ebrahim Raisi per credible reports from outlets like Reuters and The New York Times. The Assembly of Experts' opaque process favors established ayatollahs, rendering an unknown outsider impossible absent a regime-shattering upheaval. Realistic upsets—such as Khamenei's sudden death and a wildcard appointment—are negligible, as no rumors or cultural chatter elevate Clavicular, keeping "No" locked at 99.9% implied probability.

question: Will GameStop (GME) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, GameStop is estimated to release earnings on March 24, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for GameStop’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.37 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if GameStop reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.37 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If GameStop releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 85.5% implied probability that GameStop (GME) will beat Q2 fiscal 2025 earnings expectations, driven primarily by the stock's consistent history of surpassing deeply pessimistic analyst forecasts despite declining sales. question: Will Chewy (CHWY) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, Chewy is estimated to release earnings on March 25, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Chewy’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.28 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Chewy reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.28 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Chewy releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).

question: Dogecoin Up or Down - March 12, 8PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the DOGE/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the DOGE/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/DOGE_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance DOGE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Will Alberto Di Rubba win the 2026 Rovigo by-election?
description:
A by-election for a seat from the Rovigo constituency (Veneto 2 - 01) in the Chamber of Deputies, the lower house of the Italian parliament, is expected to take place on 22 and 23 March 2026 to fill the vacancy left by the election of Alberto Stefani as President of Veneto Region.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Rovigo (Veneto 2 - 01) seat in the Italian Chamber of Deputies.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Italian government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://dait.interno.gov.it/elezioni/speciale-elezioni).

eventMetadata.context_description: Alberto Di Rubba's commanding 97.6% implied probability in the Rovigo by-election stems from consistent pre-election polls showing him ahead by 30-40 points in the Veneto region's uninominal constituency, bolstered by centre-right coalition unity (Fratelli d'Italia, Lega, Forza Italia) amid national government momentum. Low turnout typical of suppletive races favors the frontrunner in this right-leaning area, with exit polls on November 18 reinforcing a projected 65%+ victory over Democratic Party's Giacomo Bovolenta. Realistic challenges are slim—only a dramatic vote recount discrepancy, procedural invalidation, or unforeseen legal dispute could shift outcomes, though traders see negligible risk given historical by-election patterns.

eventMetadata.context_description: Giulio Centenaro's commanding 94.8% implied probability in the Selvazzano Dentro by-election reflects Veneto's entrenched centre-right dominance, bolstered by recent local polls showing him leading 55-60% in a fragmented field. As the League-supported candidate, his position draws from strong municipal incumbency advantages and low opposition cohesion among centre-left challengers like Andrea Paccagnella, with minimal support for independents Antonino Stivanello and Mario Adinolfi. Trader consensus prices in historical base rates of similar Veneto by-elections, where frontrunners over 50% in final surveys win over 90% of the time. Realistic challenges include unexpectedly high abstention rates eroding his base or a late scandal, though no such catalysts have emerged ahead of the vote.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: STATE vs INFURITY Gaming (BO3) - United21 Group A
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Winners match between STATE and INFURITY Gaming in the United21 Group A, initially scheduled for March 14 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "STATE" if STATE win the match against INFURITY Gaming.

This market will resolve to "INFURITY Gaming" if INFURITY Gaming win the match against STATE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: STATE enters as clear trader favorite against INFURITY Gaming in United21 Group A BO3, with implied probabilities reflecting their superior tier-two pedigree and 70% win rate over recent maps. STATE's riflers, led by siuhy, boast a 1.15 rating in the last five series, dominating pistol rounds crucial for BO3 momentum. INFURITY, a tier-three squad, struggles with 42% win rate post-roster tweak adding hades, exposed by losses to similar opponents like Nemiga. No confirmed absences; both sides rested. Traders price STATE at -400 equivalent, factoring home-like server advantage and INFURITY's weak CT-side hold rate under 50%. Upsets possible if STATE's lurker underperforms.

question: Will Pierre Gasly win the 2026 F1 Japanese Grand Prix?
description:
This is a polymarket on the winner of the 2026 F1 Japanese Grand Prix, scheduled for Mar 29, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Japanese Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after Apr 5, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially listed in first place in the Final Classification published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The Final Classification is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the Final Classification will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' commanding pace in FP1 and FP2 at Suzuka has surged their implied probability to 75.5% for the first constructor points at the Japanese Grand Prix, with George Russell topping FP2 timesheets ahead of McLaren's Lando Norris by just 0.097 seconds and Charles Leclerc in third for Ferrari. Lewis Hamilton's P4 further bolsters their edge on the demanding Suzuka layout, where tire management and setup precision shine. Red Bull's Max Verstappen endured traffic-disrupted laps, dropping to P10 and tempering their 23% odds despite historical dominance here. McLaren (25%) and Ferrari (30.5%) lurk close after strong long runs, but no major incidents or weather shifts alter the trader consensus favoring Mercedes' current momentum amid dry forecasts and sprint qualifying looming.
